texto: We need to be wise consumers of information in an age of artificially created reality, written by professional propagandists, bought and paid for by special interests who many times disguise themselves as grassroots movements but are nothing but AstroTurf. They’ll give you turf burns and turf toe, but never the truth.
explicacao: Citação de H.L. Wegley sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “We need to be wise consumers of information in an age of artificially created reality, written by…” articula reflexao, information, internet em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como internet e news.

frase_original: We need to be wise consumers of information in an age of artificially created reality, written by professional propagandists, bought and paid for by special interests who many times disguise themselves as grassroots movements but are nothing but AstroTurf. texto: During hard times, such as serious illness, if you had to choose, which would you pick, healing or knowing God is with you? If you’re a Christian, maybe you'll get both, but knowing that, just as He said, He will be with us through anything and everything is the most comforting aspect of our God's nature. No other faith offers ‘the God who draws near’. No other faith offers the God who walked in our shoes, Jesus, the One who understands our troubles, not just by His omniscience but by actual experience.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de H.L. Wegley que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “During hard times, such as serious illness, if you had to choose, which would you pick, healing o…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, faith, god, com foco em god e jesus.
